pattern: Deletions are hard online but trivial in reverse, so processing removals backward turns each step into a union-find merge that grows segments and tracks the running maximum sum.
hints: Splitting an array by deletions is awkward; reversing time turns every deletion into an insertion that may join neighbors.
Process the removal queries from last to first, reactivating one index at a time and merging it with any already-active neighbor.

Use union-find where each root stores its segment sum; when you activate an index, union it with active neighbors, update the global max segment sum, and record that max as the answer for the step just before this insertion.

edge_inputs.description: removing the middle splits a large segment
max moves between segments as larger one shrinks

stress.description: random values with a random removal permutation
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    order = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(order)
    return [nums, order]
